title: SACRAMENTO – Assemblymember Dawn Addis (D-Morro Bay) today announced that a broad array of her bills introduced during this legislative session were approved by the State Assembly
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: California will build a strong science-based monitoring program to better understand the impact of offshore wind energy development on ocean life and habitat.
The Mobilehome Residency Law Protection Program extends the rights of vulnerable populations in California to seek justice.
California community colleges can carry out student housing plans more efficiently by streamlining the approval process.
The Biliteracy Advancement Act allows flexibility for English learners to demonstrate multi-lingual competency.
Military communities in California can access federal dollars for infrastructure improvements around military bases.
Barriers preventing minors who have experienced sexual abuse from seeking justice are being removed.
Ranchers and private landowners receive financial incentives to implement grazing practices that restore grassland, soil, and biodiversity.
Funding eligibility for Residential Care Facilities is expanded to reduce borrowing costs and support affordable senior living communities.
The CSU Tied-House Exemption Program widens sponsorship opportunities for small businesses and CSU campuses.
CSU employees are enabled to take an academic term of paid parental/family leave.
The Disability Equity and Accountability Act strengthens oversight of California regional centers to improve outcomes for individuals with developmental disabilities.
The Ocean Life Recovery Act establishes a fund targeting ocean resilience and recovery of ecosystems like kelp forests and eelgrass meadows.
